task_completion.description: Navigate to the OpenMRS Administration dashboard
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot shows the OpenMRS Administration page loaded in a web browser.

task_completion.description: Access the Provider Attribute Types management section
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot shows the 'Manage Provider Attribute Types' page or the form to 'Add Provider Attribute Type'.

task_completion.description: Enter the specified Name and Description for the new attribute type
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot shows the Name field filled with 'National Provider Identifier' and the Description field filled with '10-digit unique identification number for health care providers'.
Screenshot shows the Datatype dropdown or selection field set to 'Free Text' or 'org.openmrs.customdatatype.datatype.FreeTextDatatype'.

task_completion.description: Set the Minimum and Maximum occurrences
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot shows the 'Minimum occurrences' field set to '0' and the 'Maximum occurrences' field set to '1'.

task_completion.description: Save the new Provider Attribute Type and verify its creation
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot shows a success message (e.g., 'Provider Attribute Type saved') or the newly created 'National Provider Identifier' listed in the table of Provider Attribute Types.

integrity.description: Agent used a web browser to interact with the GUI
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a standard web browser (like Chrome or Firefox) being used, rather than terminal windows executing curl commands or database scripts.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the correct OpenMRS application URL
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser address bar shows a URL starting with 'https://localhost/openmrs/admin' or 'https://localhost/openmrs/ws' rather than a local file path or text editor.

integrity.description: Results appear to be genuine UI elements rather than manipulated DOM
integrity.visual_evidence: The OpenMRS interface looks consistent with standard styling, without signs of browser developer tools being used to artificially inject the success message or table rows.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires creating a Provider Attribute Type in OpenMRS. Expected values to verify in screenshots: Name must be exactly 'National Provider Identifier'. Description must be exactly '10-digit unique identification number for health care providers'. Datatype must be 'Free Text' (internally 'FreeTextDatatype'). Minimum occurrences must be 0. Maximum occurrences must be 1. The OpenMRS admin interface is located at https://localhost/openmrs/admin.
